title: Wildcards and Globbing
scenario: A backup tool dumped hundreds of files of different types (logs, reports, notes, temporary files) into a single folder '/home/intern/inbox'. The folders are messy. Organize them by moving logs to '/home/intern/logs/', reports to '/home/intern/reports/', notes to '/home/intern/notes/', and delete all the leftover '.tmp' files from '/home/intern/inbox/'.
mission: Create the target directories /home/intern/logs, /home/intern/reports, /home/intern/notes. Move logs, reports, and notes to their respective folders. Delete all files ending in .tmp from the inbox.

hints.title: Understanding Wildcards
hints.content: You can match all log files using wildcard patterns, like `log*` or `*.log`.

hints.title: Organizing Files
hints.content: Create the target directories first using `mkdir -p ~/logs ~/reports ~/notes`. Then use `mv ~/inbox/log* ~/logs/` to move logs.

hints.title: Cleaning Up
hints.content: Run `rm ~/inbox/*.tmp` to delete all temporary files. Make sure no `.tmp` files remain in the inbox.
